event: Alarm rang 13 minutes early
detail: Sleep tracker detected a light sleep cycle and triggered the alarm early to avoid grogginess.
label: 🔴 The Optimizer
toggle_off: Rahul wakes at 7:00 AM naturally. He feels slightly groggy but chooses when to start his day.
source: Fitbit, Samsung Health sleep algorithm documentation

event: First news headline shown was about a political controversy
detail: The algorithm predicted high engagement probability based on his reading history. Outrage content gets 2.3x more clicks.
label: 🔵 The Engager
toggle_off: Rahul sees a chronological feed. He reads about local weather and a science discovery.
source: Facebook internal research leak, 2021. MIT Media Lab engagement study.

event: YouTube autosuggested a 22-minute video instead of a 4-minute one
detail: Longer watch time increases session duration. The algorithm surfaces longer content even when shorter content answers the query better.
label: 🔵 The Engager
toggle_off: Rahul watches the 4-minute video. Gets the answer. Moves on.
source: YouTube recommendation system research, Guillaume Chaslot (former YouTube engineer)

event: Grocery app showed premium brand first
detail: The top result wasn't the best-reviewed product. It was the one that paid for placement.
label: 🟠 The Monetizer
toggle_off: Results sorted by rating. The best-reviewed product appears first.
source: Amazon paid placement documentation, Consumer Reports investigation 2022

event: Spotify played a song from a label that paid for promotion
detail: Spotify's algorithm gives playlist placement advantages to labels that pay for distribution services. Independent artists get buried.
label: 🟠 The Monetizer
toggle_off: Playlist curated purely by listening history. An independent artist Rahul would love appears instead.
source: Spotify Loud & Clear report, Music Business Worldwide investigation 2023

event: Weather app showed a sponsored umbrella ad before the forecast
detail: The app monetizes through contextual advertising. Your data — including location and weather queries — is sold to advertisers in real time.
label: 🟠 The Monetizer
toggle_off: Rahul opens the app. Sees the weather immediately.
source: Weather Channel app data monetization investigation, LA Times 2019

event: Job application rejected in 11 seconds
detail: An ATS (Applicant Tracking System) scanned the resume. A required keyword was missing. No human ever saw it.
label: 🟡 The Gatekeeper
toggle_off: Resume reaches a human recruiter. They notice the relevant experience despite the missing keyword.
source: Harvard Business School report: Hidden Workers, 2021. 75% of resumes rejected by ATS before human review.

event: LinkedIn showed Rahul jobs he's overqualified for
detail: The recommendation model is optimised for engagement (clicks), not fit. Overqualified candidates click more to check if the role is real.
label: 🔵 The Engager
toggle_off: Jobs matched by actual skill-level fit. Results are fewer but relevant.
source: LinkedIn algorithm analysis, Reuters 2022

event: Email inbox sorted a genuine message into Promotions
detail: Gmail's classification model flagged a small business invoice as promotional content. The sender's domain had low trust score.
label: 🟡 The Gatekeeper
toggle_off: Email arrives in primary inbox. Rahul sees it immediately.
source: Google Gmail classification system documentation

event: Maps app routed Rahul past a sponsored petrol station
detail: Google Maps accepts payment from fuel partners to influence routing. The route was 400 metres longer.
label: 🟠 The Monetizer
toggle_off: Shortest route shown. No sponsored detour.
source: Google Maps local advertising documentation, The Markup investigation 2020

event: A dark pattern on a subscription page pre-ticked the annual plan
detail: The default was set to the most expensive tier. Most users don't notice the pre-selection.
label: 🟠 The Monetizer
toggle_off: Default is the basic free tier. Rahul upgrades only if he chooses to.
source: Deceptive Design Patterns research, NNGroup 2022

event: Content feed showed him the same opinion three times from different accounts
detail: Engagement clustering: content that generated interaction gets amplified. Three accounts shared the same article. Algorithm treated each as fresh signal.
label: 🔵 The Engager
toggle_off: Feed shows diverse perspectives. Rahul encounters a view that challenges his own.
source: Filter bubble research, Eli Pariser. Echo chamber study, MIT 2022.

event: Autocomplete suggested a biased phrasing in his search
detail: Google autocomplete is trained on historical search data. Biased societal patterns in past searches get surfaced as suggestions.
label: ⚫ The Invisible One
toggle_off: No suggestion. Rahul types his own query.
source: Research by Safiya Umoja Noble: Algorithms of Oppression

event: A chatbot gave confident medical information that was outdated
detail: The model had a knowledge cutoff. It didn't know the guidance had changed. It answered confidently anyway.
label: ⚫ The Invisible One
toggle_off: Rahul consults a doctor or a current medical source.
source: JAMA Network Open study on AI medical misinformation, 2023

event: Video call background blur failed to blur his flatmate
detail: The segmentation model wasn't trained on diverse skin tones. It performs worse on darker complexions.
label: ⚫ The Invisible One
toggle_off: Background blur works correctly for everyone on the call.
source: AI bias in computer vision research, MIT Media Lab Gender Shades study

event: A sponsored café appeared at the top of his search despite lower ratings
detail: Google Local ranking mixes organic ranking with paid placement. The paid café had 3.9 stars. The organic result had 4.6.
label: 🟠 The Monetizer
toggle_off: Results sorted by rating. Best-reviewed café appears first.
source: Google Local Services Ads documentation, The Markup 2020

event: Delivery app showed him a restaurant 2.3 km further than a closer alternative
detail: The closer restaurant hadn't paid for premium placement. The farther one had. Delivery time estimate was also algorithmically smoothed to look similar.
label: 🟠 The Monetizer
toggle_off: Results sorted by distance. Closest restaurant appears first.
source: Zomato and Swiggy platform ranking investigations, The Ken 2022

event: The restaurant review he read was AI-generated
detail: A 5-star review posted three weeks ago. Grammatically perfect. Emotionally flat. No verifiable visit. The platform's moderation didn't catch it.
label: ⚫ The Invisible One
toggle_off: Only verified reviews shown. The café has 23 real reviews, averaging 3.8 stars.
source: FTC warnings on AI-generated reviews 2023, Which? investigation UK

event: Payment app offered a loan at 28% interest as the first option
detail: The highest-margin product for the platform was placed first. A lower-rate option existed but required scrolling.
label: 🟠 The Monetizer
toggle_off: Loan options sorted by interest rate, lowest first.
source: RBI digital lending guidelines 2022, NPCI fintech design pattern analysis

event: His screen time notification was dismissed automatically
detail: The app learned that Rahul always dismisses screen time warnings. It reduced the frequency. Fewer interruptions = more usage.
label: 🔵 The Engager
toggle_off: Notification appears at full frequency. Rahul puts his phone down.
source: Apple Screen Time algorithm research, Center for Humane Technology

event: A 'limited time offer' countdown timer was fake
detail: The timer reset every time the page was refreshed. A standard dark pattern. Still legal in India.
label: 🟠 The Monetizer
toggle_off: No fake urgency. Rahul evaluates the product on its merits.
source: Deceptive design patterns research, darkpatterns.org, Consumer Forum India

event: Instagram Reels played 11 videos after he intended to stop
detail: Autoplay with no friction. The end of one video is the start of the next. There is no natural stopping point by design.
label: 🔵 The Engager
toggle_off: Videos require a manual tap to continue. Rahul watches two and closes the app.
source: Instagram internal engagement research leak via Frances Haugen, 2021

event: His credit score was checked without his knowledge
detail: A fintech app he used performed a soft credit inquiry in the background when he browsed loan options. He was never notified.
label: ⚫ The Invisible One
toggle_off: No background credit check. Data stays with Rahul unless he explicitly consents.
source: RBI data privacy guidelines, Fintech credit access research India 2023

event: A price he saw earlier had increased by ₹180
detail: Dynamic pricing algorithm detected he had visited the product page three times. Repeat visits signal high purchase intent. Price adjusted accordingly.
label: 🟠 The Monetizer
toggle_off: Price is static. Rahul pays the same amount regardless of how many times he looked.
source: Amazon and Flipkart dynamic pricing investigations, Indian Express 2022

event: A voice assistant misheard him and placed a wrong order
detail: The speech recognition model had lower accuracy for his regional accent. It confirmed the wrong order confidently.
label: ⚫ The Invisible One
toggle_off: The assistant asks for confirmation before placing any order.
source: Stanford HAI accent bias in speech recognition research, 2020

event: An ad followed him across three different apps
detail: Cross-app tracking via device fingerprinting. Even without cookies, advertisers can identify him across platforms.
label: 🟠 The Monetizer
toggle_off: No cross-app tracking. Each app sees only its own data.
source: EFF research on device fingerprinting, Apple ATT framework documentation

event: A content moderation system removed his post within 40 seconds
detail: His post contained a phrase that triggered an automated filter. No human reviewed it. No appeal option was visible.
label: 🟡 The Gatekeeper
toggle_off: Post goes live. If reported, a human moderator reviews it within 24 hours.
source: Facebook content moderation transparency report, 2023. ACLU automated moderation research.

event: A map showed him a road that no longer exists
detail: The map data was 14 months old. A construction closure wasn't updated in time. The routing algorithm had no real-time data.
label: ⚫ The Invisible One
toggle_off: Real-time data integration flags the closed road. Rahul takes a different route.
source: Google Maps data freshness research, OSM community reports India

event: His photo was auto-tagged by facial recognition without consent
detail: A social platform's facial recognition model identified him in a friend's photo and suggested tagging. The model processes biometric data by default.
label: ⚫ The Invisible One
toggle_off: No auto-tagging. Facial recognition requires explicit opt-in.
source: GDPR facial recognition rulings, India DPDP Act 2023 biometric provisions

event: A productivity app sent 4 notifications in 40 minutes
detail: Notification frequency is optimised for re-engagement, not user productivity. Each re-open counts as a daily active user event.
label: 🔵 The Engager
toggle_off: Notifications sent only when genuinely urgent. Rahul gets one.
source: Hooked by Nir Eyal (product design), Center for Humane Technology reports

event: A recommended 'friend' was actually a data broker profile
detail: People You May Know algorithms surface connections based on shared data. Some profiles exist to harvest connection data, not connect socially.
label: ⚫ The Invisible One
toggle_off: Recommendations based only on verified mutual connections.
source: Gizmodo PYMK investigation, Kashmir Hill reporting on Facebook data graph

event: An insurance app quoted him higher because of his PIN code
detail: Location-based pricing is legal and common. Living in a certain area increases your premium even if you personally have no claims history.
label: 🟡 The Gatekeeper
toggle_off: Premium calculated on personal claims history only.
source: ProPublica insurance algorithm discrimination research, IRDAI India pricing model analysis

event: YouTube autoplay started a 90-minute documentary
detail: Rahul intended to watch a 12-minute video. Autoplay triggered immediately after. He watched 47 minutes before noticing.
label: 🔵 The Engager
toggle_off: Autoplay is off by default. Rahul's next video requires a deliberate tap.
source: Center for Humane Technology, YouTube autoplay impact studies

event: A translation app mistranslated a formal document
detail: Neural machine translation models perform well on common language pairs but struggle with legal and technical terminology. The error wasn't flagged.
label: ⚫ The Invisible One
toggle_off: Rahul uses a human translator for the formal document.
source: ACL research on NMT errors in legal documents, 2022

event: A food delivery estimate was 12 minutes off
detail: ETA algorithms are optimised to reduce cancellations, not to be accurate. A slightly optimistic estimate keeps the order alive.
label: 🔴 The Optimizer
toggle_off: Accurate ETA shown. Rahul decides whether to wait or cook.
source: Swiggy and Zomato ETA accuracy analysis, The Ken 2021

event: His phone suggested a contact to call that he hasn't spoken to in 2 years
detail: The suggestion algorithm detected a calendar event that might be related. It cross-referenced email metadata, location, and call history.
label: ⚫ The Invisible One
toggle_off: No cross-app inference. Suggestions come only from recent call history.
source: Google Assistant data integration documentation, Android privacy research

event: A streaming service played a show he didn't choose
detail: Autoplay after episode end. The next episode loaded in 5 seconds with no opt-out clearly visible. He watched two more episodes than he intended.
label: 🔵 The Engager
toggle_off: Autoplay requires confirmation after each episode. Rahul watches one and stops.
source: Netflix autoplay default settings, EU digital services regulation on dark patterns

event: A chatbot resolved his complaint as 'solved' without solving it
detail: The bot detected keywords associated with resolution in his last message. It closed the ticket. The problem remained.
label: 🔴 The Optimizer
toggle_off: Ticket stays open until Rahul explicitly confirms resolution.
source: Customer service automation research, Gartner 2022

event: An app shared his location with 14 third parties
detail: The permission said 'location access for better experience'. The privacy policy, on page 11, listed 14 data partners. Nobody reads page 11.
label: ⚫ The Invisible One
toggle_off: Location shared only with the app itself. Rahul controls who else sees it.
source: ICCL report on mobile data brokers 2023, India DPDP Act provisions

event: A mental health app sold anonymised data to an insurance company
detail: The data was 'anonymised'. Re-identification from anonymised datasets takes an average of 3 additional data points. The insurance company had many.
label: ⚫ The Invisible One
toggle_off: Mental health data never leaves the app or its servers.
source: Psychiatric data monetization investigation, STAT News 2021. De-anonymisation research, Nature 2019.

event: A game sent him a notification at exactly the time he usually plays
detail: Behavioural prediction model identified his daily gaming window. Notification sent 8 minutes before to trigger the habit loop.
label: 🔵 The Engager
toggle_off: No predictive notifications. Rahul opens the game when he chooses.
source: Hooked model by Nir Eyal, behavioural design in mobile gaming research

event: A social media algorithm showed him a post from 3 days ago as if it was new
detail: The post had high engagement. The algorithm re-surfaces high-performing content without labelling it as old. Rahul responded thinking it was current.
label: 🔵 The Engager
toggle_off: Posts labelled with actual date. Rahul knows the context before responding.
source: Twitter/X and Instagram non-chronological feed research, Reuters Institute 2022

event: An e-commerce return was denied by an automated system
detail: The system flagged his account as high-return-risk based on purchase pattern. The return was legitimate. No human reviewed the flag.
label: 🟡 The Gatekeeper
toggle_off: Return request reviewed by a human. Approved within 24 hours.
source: Amazon automated return denial reports, Consumer complaints India 2023

event: His reading speed was inferred and used to adjust ad timing
detail: The app measured scroll velocity to estimate reading speed. Ads were injected at the point of maximum attention — just before the key paragraph.
label: 🟠 The Monetizer
toggle_off: Ads appear at fixed intervals, not attention-optimised moments.
source: Attention economy research, Tim Wu 'The Attention Merchants'

event: A 'do not disturb' mode was overridden by a promotional notification
detail: The app had a special permission category that bypassed system-level DND. This was buried in the notification settings, pre-enabled.
label: 🔵 The Engager
toggle_off: DND means DND. No app overrides it without explicit user permission.
source: Android notification channel abuse research, XDA Developers community analysis

event: Sleep tracking started without being asked
detail: The watch detected he was still. Sleep tracking activated automatically. Heart rate, movement, and breathing patterns recorded all night.
label: 🔴 The Optimizer
toggle_off: Sleep tracking only activates when manually started.
source: Fitbit, Garmin, Apple Watch passive data collection documentation

event: His sleep data was processed and sold to a wellness data aggregator
detail: The health app's terms of service — accepted without reading — permitted third-party data sharing for 'research and wellness purposes'. The aggregator resells to insurers.
label: ⚫ The Invisible One
toggle_off: Health data stays on device. No third-party sharing ever.
source: Axios health data investigation 2023, India DPDP Act health data provisions
